value.found_quote: In this guide, we will focus on configuring evals programmatically using the Evals API. If you prefer, you can also configure evals in the OpenAI dashboard. OpenAI is deprecating the Evals platform. Existing evals content remains available during the transition window. Evals will become read-only for existing users on October 31, 2026, and the platform is scheduled to shut down on November 30, 2026.
value.rationale: The page fully substantiates the claim. It confirms: (1) an active Evals API exists for programmatically creating, configuring, and running evaluations of model outputs asynchronously at scale (with queued/async run execution and result tracking); (2) a dashboard-based Evals platform also exists; and (3) the legacy Evals platform is explicitly deprecated with a scheduled shutdown — read-only on October 31, 2026 and full shutdown on November 30, 2026. The Evals API itself remains active while the platform (dashboard) is deprecated/sunset-scheduled.
